scope: Educational exploration of population-level transmission dynamics and parameter sensitivity.
limitations: Not intended to represent any specific real-world condition.

description: Reference respiratory transmission scenario based on the project COVID-19 benchmark configuration.
scope: Illustrative population-level scenario aligned with the project reference comparator.
limitations: Simplified representation. Does not reproduce any specific jurisdiction, period or outbreak.

description: Seasonal respiratory transmission example for aggregate population-level dynamics.
scope: Editable literature-informed respiratory transmission profile for teaching, planning and scenario comparison. Parameters are simplified defaults, not a reconstruction of a specific season, geography or strain.
assumptions: SEIRD-compatible aggregate defaults use peer-reviewed evidence on reproduction number, Influenza A incubation period, shedding/infectious timeline and seasonal influenza mortality. The mortality parameter uses the median seasonal influenza infection fatality risk from Filipe et al. 2025, converted from 50.05 deaths per 100,000 infections to 0.05005%. This is an aggregate IFR-based value, not a symptomatic CFR. Values remain review_pending until formal parameter review.
limitations: Simplified aggregate profile. Does not model strain-specific dynamics, vaccination, age-specific immunity, healthcare access, antigenic drift, jurisdiction-specific surveillance artefacts, or the distinction between infection fatality risk and symptomatic case fatality risk beyond the documented aggregate mortality default.

description: High-transmission respiratory example for aggregate population-level dynamics.
scope: Editable literature-informed profile illustrating high R0 respiratory transmission and sensitivity to susceptible population structure. Not a historical reconstruction.
assumptions: SEIRD-compatible aggregate defaults use peer-reviewed evidence on R0, incubation, infectious period and case fatality. Values remain review_pending until formal parameter review.
limitations: Simplified aggregate profile. Does not model vaccination coverage, herd immunity thresholds, age-specific susceptibility, outbreak control, underascertainment or local health-system context.

description: Regional-contact severe transmission example for aggregate population-level dynamics.
scope: Editable literature-informed profile for exploring severe regional-contact transmission patterns in an aggregated model. Not a reconstruction of a specific outbreak.
assumptions: SEIRD-compatible aggregate defaults use peer-reviewed evidence on reproduction number, incubation period, infectiousness timeline and case fatality. Values remain review_pending until formal parameter review.
limitations: Simplified aggregate profile. Does not model household/caregiving networks, burial practices, isolation delays, treatment access, reporting delays or outbreak-specific response capacity.

description: Water-associated regional transmission example for aggregate population-level dynamics.
scope: Editable literature-informed profile for water-associated transmission dynamics in an aggregated scenario framework. Not a setting-specific environmental model.
assumptions: SEIRD-compatible aggregate defaults use peer-reviewed evidence on reproduction number, incubation period, shedding/infectious timeline and fatality estimates. Values remain review_pending until formal parameter review.
limitations: Simplified aggregate profile. Does not explicitly model water systems, sanitation, bacterial concentration, oral rehydration access, climate, seasonality or spatial environmental reservoirs.

description: Slow-progression transmission example for aggregate population-level dynamics.
scope: Editable literature-informed profile illustrating slow progression and longer infectious timelines in an aggregated scenario framework. Not a full tuberculosis natural-history model.
assumptions: SEIRD-compatible aggregate defaults use peer-reviewed evidence on reproduction number, latent-to-active progression assumptions, disease duration and mortality. Values remain review_pending until formal parameter review.
limitations: Highly simplified aggregate profile. Does not model latent infection reservoirs, reactivation over years, treatment, drug resistance, HIV status, contact heterogeneity or detailed natural history.
